body_txt: A fair-weather friend is someone who is happy to be with you when everything is going well but will abandon you when something bad happens. Friends are good, especially when they are True Companions who will stick with you through thick and thin. But not all of your companions will actually prove true. Some of them will turn their backs on you the moment you actually need their help . Hence the name: a fair-weather friend vanishes like the sun when being your friend isn't fun any more. Sometimes, this character will bookend the story, hanging out with the protagonist at the beginning, disappearing when difficulties arise, then coming back when the trouble is over and acting as if nothing had happened. Often a part of an Ineffectual Loner 's backstory. Not exactly the same as a False Friend : a Fair-Weather Friend genuinely likes you — at first — but they'll skip out as soon as friendship with you becomes inconvenient. Can overlap with Friendship Bribe , where someone will be your friend as long as you buy them things. Inverse of Power of Friendship . Contrast A Friend in Need . Do not confuse with Social Circle Filler , who are friends that drop out of the story after the main character gets better, more plot-relevant ones (which would effectively make the main character one of these). Sister Trope to Fair-Weather Foe . Platonic counterpart to Fair-Weather Ex . Compare this to With Friends Like These... and I Fight for the Strongest Side! , which often overlap with this trope. Contrast Financial Test of Friendship . See also Fair-Weather Mentor , for when it's a teacher who takes this attitude.
